name: TAO Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: TAO 4h flushes can create tradable continuation only after inventory resets and price reclaims structure, rather than on raw oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from reclaim-after-flush behavior with broader participation thresholds.
invalidation_condition: Abort if this remains too sparse, structurally contradictory, or cannot produce enough blind-window trades after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Flush-and-reclaim behavior where inventory resets create fuel for continuation rather than pure bounce-and-fade.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When a sharp flush is reclaimed quickly, wrong-footed inventory can create follow-through beyond a simple mean-reversion bounce.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is volatile enough for inventory resets to be meaningful while still allowing multiple reclaim tests over a backtest window.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If reclaim moves are too brief or the market stays fully range-bound, continuation after reset may not materialize.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If some trades appear but density is weak, reduce reclaim distance requirements before changing trade management.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and directional confirmation align after the reset
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad flush threshold and simple qualifier stack

long: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
short: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
